_publications._publicationTitle: Development of an adeno-associated virus vector for gene replacement therapy of NF1-related tumors.
_publications._context: Human MPNST cell line ST88-14, S462 and a patient-derived xenoline RHT92 were kindly provided by Melissa L. Fishel at the Indiana University School of Medicine | We maintained MPNST cell lines ST88-14, STS26T, S520, S462, 88-3, 90-8 and YST1 as described previously | malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumour (MPNST) cell lines ST88-14, S462 and NF90-8 | cells were seeded at 1.0 × 104 (S462 cell-line) or 3.0 × 104 (2884 and 2885 cell-lines) cells/well | Human MPNST cell lines S462, STS-26T, ST88-14, and 90-8 and an immortalized normal human Schwann cell line (iHSCλ2) were obtained from Nancy Ratner | MPNST cell lines STS26T, ST8814, ST88-3 S462, T265p21, S520, 90–8, and YST1 | Authenticated human MPNST cell lines (sporadic STS26T, called 26T, Cellosaurus RRID: CVCL_8917; NF1-associated S462, Cellosaurus RRID: CVCL_1Y70) | MPNST cell lines (STS-26T, S462, S462TY, T265) were obtained from Dr. David Largaespada's laboratory | S462 cells were human NF1–related MPNST cells (SCC414, MilliporeSigma). | S462 (RRID: CVCL_1Y70; ref. 24), sNF96.2 (RRID: CVCL_K281; ref. 25), ST88-14 (RRID: CVCL_8916; ref. 26) | Human MPNST cell lines (S462, T265, and NSF1) were analyzed and have been described in detail in our previous studies | Human MPNST cell lines S462 and T265 were described previously...S462 but not T265 cells form solid subcutaneous tumors | human MPNST cell lines (ST88-14, STS26T, S462, S462-TY, and T265) were cultured in complete media | ST-8814 and S462 cells were obtained from Dr. Andrew Tee (Cardiff University)
